title: How to Remove Duplicates in Google Sheets
dek: To remove duplicates in Google Sheets, select your data and go to Data > Data cleanup > Remove duplicates. To keep your original list untouched, use the UNIQUE function instead. Here is how to do both.

intent.primaryKeyword: how to remove duplicates in google sheets

seo.metaTitle: How to Remove Duplicates in Google Sheets
seo.metaDescription: Remove duplicates in Google Sheets with Data cleanup, or keep your original list with the UNIQUE function. Step-by-step guide.

sources.label: Google Docs Editors Help — Clean up your data

richText: Duplicate rows throw off counts and totals in Google Sheets just like anywhere else. There are two clean ways to handle them: delete them in place, or build a fresh duplicate-free list with a formula.

items: Select the range that includes your data (with headers).
Tick "Data has header row" if it does, and choose which columns to compare.
Click Remove duplicates. Sheets tells you how many rows it removed.

body.richText: This deletes rows for good. Make a copy of the sheet first (right-click the tab > Duplicate), or be ready to press Ctrl+Z.

body.text: Method 2: The UNIQUE function (keeps your original)

richText: To get a deduplicated list without deleting anything, use UNIQUE in an empty cell:

body.richText: The list updates automatically as your source data changes, which makes it ideal for live dashboards. You can also wrap it in SORT to get a clean, ordered list: =SORT(UNIQUE(A2:A100)).

items.question: Does Remove duplicates in Google Sheets keep the first row?
items.answerRichText: Yes, it keeps the first occurrence and removes later matching rows. Sort your data first if you need a specific row to be the one that stays.

items.question: How do I highlight duplicates instead of removing them?
items.answerRichText: Use Format > Conditional formatting with a custom formula like =COUNTIF(A:A, A1)>1. Google Sheets then colors every value that appears more than once.
